summary: Backend-only testing for Medify MVP completed. 28/28 pytest tests pass (100%) against external URL https://medify-health-1.preview.emergentagent.com/api. Covered patient CRUD, medicines + logs + adherence, prescriptions incl. auto-medicine creation and vision scan, chat (EN + BN + history persistence + clear), appointments, health records (with image_base64), hospitals (sorted), emergency contacts (999/16263/10921 present), SOS (notifies guardian+doctor). All responses verified free of MongoDB _id. Fixed one backend bug: /api/prescriptions/scan was returning 502 when OpenRouter rejected an image; now correctly returns 200 with ok=false fallback schema per spec.

minor.issue: Empty strings are skipped due to `v != ""` filter, so clearing an instructions/dosage field is impossible via update. Consider only filtering None.

Logic in lines 286-288 maps slot index i -> defaults[slots[i]] but skips slots with '0'. For frequency '0-0-1' it would map to slots[0]='morning' (wrong); only works because filter happens before mapping. Verify with '0-1-0' which currently picks 'morning' instead of 'noon'.

When auto-creating medicines from prescription, frequency 'startswith("1")' adds 08:00 for ANY pattern starting with 1 (e.g. '1-0-0' would also match endswith('1') giving wrong evening slot). Frequency pattern parsing diverges from /api/medicines.

react_native_issues.issue: Expo bundler ERROR observed in /var/log/supervisor/expo.err.log: 'import * as NativeSvg from "react-native-svg"' fails. Import chain: app/emergency.tsx -> lucide-react-native -> react-native-svg. react-native-svg is likely missing from package.json (frontend testing was OUT OF SCOPE but reporting since logs show bundler crash).

action_items: Install react-native-svg (yarn add react-native-svg) so lucide-react-native icons load on the Expo web bundle; emergency.tsx currently breaks the bundler.

Consider unifying frequency-to-times parsing between POST /api/medicines and the auto-medicine logic inside POST /api/prescriptions; current divergence can produce wrong times for patterns like '0-1-0' or '1-0-0'.
PUT /api/medicines should allow clearing string fields (currently filters out empty strings).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 703 lines - consider splitting into routers (patient, medicines, prescriptions, chat, appointments, records, emergency) for maintainability.
Hardcoded DEMO_PATIENT_ID is acceptable for MVP but should be moved to config when multi-user support is added.

/api/chat trims history to last 10 docs but uses sort desc then reverse - efficient; however it includes the system role implicitly so 10 messages per session may be insufficient for longer conversations - consider 20.
OpenRouter API key is committed in /app/backend/.env. Acceptable for MVP/test environment but should be rotated before any public release.
Scan endpoint now correctly returns 200 with ok=false fallback for all OpenRouter failures (timeout, HTTP error, parse error) - fixed in this iteration.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (fixed scan_prescription HTTPStatusError handler to return 200 with ok=false fallback instead of raising 502)
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed required - backend auto-seeds patient (Rahima Begum), 6 emergency contacts, 7 hospitals on startup. Test data is TEST_ prefixed and cleaned up by each test class.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend API at https://medify-health-1.preview.emergentagent.com/api - all 28 endpoints pass via /app/backend/tests/test_medify_api.py. OpenRouter free tier may 429 - chat tests already retry. Frontend bundler currently crashes due to missing react-native-svg (lucide-react-native dep) - not tested.
rca_of_issue: Scan endpoint bug RCA: /api/prescriptions/scan caught httpx.HTTPStatusError separately and raised HTTPException(502) instead of returning ok=false fallback. When OpenRouter rejects a small/invalid image with 400, the client received 502 - violating the spec ('must return 200 with ok flag and extracted schema'). Mitigation applied: collapsed HTTPStatusError handler to return the same fallback dict as the generic Exception path.
